generatedSentence: Our protagonist, a nebbish scholar from a faraway university in Japan, felt blind to the larger world, his perspective limited to dusty tomes. He often spent long summers in a decaying treehouse, a unitary place that served as his sanctuary. He was determined to capitalize on his intellect, even if others would reject him as a simpleton. His grandma, a wise old mammal and a grower of lentil crops, lovingly offered him alimentation advice, urging him to look beyond his understated hairstyle. He often dreamt of numerous adventures beyond the globe. One nightly visit to a jampacked nightclub revealed a tricky plot: gamblers were using narcotic-laced whisky to orchestrate a vindictive act against newsmakers. Their miscellaneous vehicles were filled with janky, interlocked devices. The protagonist, adopting a nonchalant uniform, decided to sneak around the periphery. He understood that these violent individuals, resembling puppeteers, had acquired a powerful, nameless weapon. His bloodstream pulsed, realizing this was no simple hallucination, but a calculated threat that could petrify the entire globe. He manhandled a guard, seizing a sharp knife and buckles. The ultraviolet glow of the countdown on the giant device, resembling a jumbo placeholder, warned of an impending earthquake. He gawked at the ornaments powering it, a hornet-like drone buzzing around, making a peculiar octave sound. A prosecutor, flanked by a nimrod simpleton, delivered keynotes, reveling in the limitless destruction. The protagonist’s journey led him through a flattened canyon, past a defunct windmill, realizing this was a needless, total wipeout. He knew he had to act, for the fate of twelve chickens and all life. With added resolve, his newfound superpower of perfect anatomical analysis allowed him to thoroughly dismantle the device. The aging puppeteer, a journalist for the newsmakers, was exposed, his idealism of redefining ownership now a cynical giveback. The protagonist, having a meaningful understanding, became a hero, weathering the storm. He secured the place, ensuring respect and kindness would prevail. The temperature normalized, the threat gone, and the world was safe, a silent rallye of gratitude echoing around him, his quiet heroism understood.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A young scholar wearing a simple, patched tunic and large, round spectacles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The scholar sits studiously within a rustic treehouse, a pile of ancient books beside him, looking longingly at a holographic globe, wide angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The scholar, now in a dark, sleek uniform, stealthily navigates a jampacked, neon-lit nightclub filled with shadowy figures, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The scholar confronts a giant, glowing countdown device in a desolate, canyon-like landscape, a hovering hornet-like drone nearby, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The scholar stands triumphantly over the dismantled device, a powerful energy surge radiating from his hands, looking determined and resolute, full body shot.

definitions.definition: One who is fearful and timid, especially in making decisions and plans, in discussions, debates, arguments, and confrontations, and in taking responsibility.
definitions.example: Actor Rick Moranis often plays a nebbish.

definitions.definition: A covering for a window to keep out light. The covering may be made of cloth or of narrow slats that can block light or allow it to pass.
A destination sign mounted on a public transport vehicle displaying the route destination, number, name and/or via points, etc.
Any device intended to conceal or hide.
definitions.example: a duck blind

definitions.definition: Something to mislead the eye or the understanding, or to conceal some covert deed or design; a subterfuge.
A blindage.
A halting place.
The blindside.
(1800s) No score.
A forced bet: the small blind or the big blind.
definitions.example: The blinds are $10/$20 and the ante is $1.

definitions.definition: A player who is forced to pay such a bet.
definitions.example: The blinds immediately folded when I reraised.

definitions.definition: (as a plural) Those who are blind, taken as a group.
To make temporarily or permanently blind.
definitions.example: Don't wave that pencil in my face - do you want to blind me?

definitions.definition: To curse.
To darken; to obscure to the eye or understanding; to conceal.
To cover with a thin coating of sand and fine gravel, for example a road newly paved, in order that the joints between the stones may be filled.
(of a person or animal) Unable to see, due to physiological or neurological factors.
(of an eye) Unable to be used to see, due to physiological or neurological factors.
Failing to see, acknowledge, perceive.
definitions.example: Authors are blind to their own defects.

definitions.definition: Of a place, having little or no visibility.
definitions.example: a blind corner

definitions.definition: Closed at one end; having a dead end
definitions.example: a blind gut

definitions.definition: Having no openings for light or passage.
definitions.example: a blind alley

definitions.definition: (in certain phrases) Smallest or slightest.
definitions.example: I shouted, but he didn't take a blind bit of notice.

definitions.definition: Without any prior knowledge.
definitions.example: He took a blind guess at which fork in the road would take him to the airport.

definitions.definition: Unconditional; without regard to evidence, logic, reality, accidental mistakes, extenuating circumstances, etc.
definitions.example: blind deference

definitions.definition: Unintelligible or illegible.
definitions.example: a blind passage in a book; blind writing

definitions.definition: Abortive; failing to produce flowers or fruit.
definitions.example: blind buds

definitions.definition: Without seeing; unseeingly.
Absolutely, totally.
definitions.example: to swear blind

definitions.definition: (three card brag) Without looking at the cards dealt.
In writing or editing, to write (something: either an entire word or text, or just the initial letter(s) thereof) in capital letters, in upper case.
definitions.example: In German, all nouns are capitalized.

definitions.definition: To contribute or acquire capital (money or other resources) for.
definitions.example: Some states require proof that a new venture is properly capitalized before the state will issue a certificate of incorporation.

definitions.definition: To convert into capital, i.e., to get cash or similar immediately fungible resources for some less fungible property or source of future income.
definitions.example: If we obtain a loan using the business as collateral, the effect will be to capitalize our next ten years of income, giving us cash today that we can use to buy out our competitor.

definitions.definition: To treat as capital, not as an expense.
To profit or to obtain an advantage.
definitions.example: The home team took several shots on goal but was unable to capitalize until late in the game.

definitions.definition: (followed by on) To seize, as an opportunity; to obtain a benefit; to invest on something profitable.
definitions.example: The home team appeared to have the advantage throughout the game, and finally capitalized on their opponents' weakness with just two minutes remaining, scoring several points in quick succession.

definitions.definition: An animal of the class Mammalia, characterized by being warm-blooded, having hair and feeding milk to its young.
A vertebrate with three bones in the inner ear and one in the jaw.
Something that is rejected.
An unpopular person.
A rejected defective product in a production line
To refuse to accept.
definitions.example: She even rejected my improved offer.

definitions.definition: To block a shot, especially if it sends the ball off the court.
To refuse a romantic advance.
definitions.example: I've been rejected three times this week.

definitions.definition: One who sneaks; one who moves stealthily to acquire an item or information.
definitions.example: My little brother is such a sneak; yesterday I caught him trying to look through my diary.

definitions.definition: A cheat; a con artist.
definitions.example: I can't believe I gave that sneak $50 for a ticket when they were selling for $20 at the front gate.

definitions.definition: An informer; a tell-tale.
A ball bowled so as to roll along the ground; a daisy-cutter
A sneaker; a tennis shoe.
A play where the quarterback receives the snap and immediately dives forward.
To creep or go stealthily; to come or go while trying to avoid detection, as a person who does not wish to be seen.
definitions.example: He decided to sneak into the kitchen for a second cookie while his mom was on the phone.

definitions.definition: To take something stealthily without permission.
definitions.example: I went to sneak a chocolate but my dad caught me.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To stealthily bring someone something.
definitions.example: She asked me to sneak her a phone next month.

definitions.definition: To hide, especially in a mean or cowardly manner.
(with on) To inform an authority of another's misdemeanours.
definitions.example: If you sneak on me I'll bash you!

definitions.definition: In advance; before release to the general public.
definitions.example: The company gave us a sneak look at their new electronic devices.

definitions.definition: In a stealthy or surreptitious manner.
definitions.example: I was able to get a sneak peek at the guest list.

definitions.definition: Hard to deal with, complicated
definitions.example: They were in a tricky situation.

definitions.definition: Adept at using deception
definitions.example: A tricky salesman can sell anything.

definitions.definition: Relating to or associated with a prostitution trick
definitions.example: I don't want any of your tricky money, thank you!

definitions.definition: In a loving manner, affectionately.

Any substance or drug that reduces pain, induces sleep and may alter mood or behaviour; in some contexts, especially in reference to the opiates-and-opioids class, especially in reference to illegal drugs, and often both.
Any type of numbing drug.
Of, or relating to narcotics.
(pharmaceutical effect) Inducing sleep; causing narcosis.
A domestic fowl, Gallus gallus, especially when young.
The meat from this bird eaten as food.
The young of any bird; a chick.
A coward.
A young or inexperienced person.
A young, attractive, slim man, usually having little body hair; compare chickenhawk.
The game of dare.
A simple dance in which the movements of a chicken are imitated.
To avoid a situation one is afraid of.
Feeding, being fed; the provision of food and other necessities.

One of four seasons, traditionally the second, marked by the longest and typically hottest days of the year due to the inclination of the Earth and thermal lag. Typically regarded as being from June 21 to September 22 or 23 in parts of the USA, the months of June, July and August in the United Kingdom and the months of December, January and February in the Southern Hemisphere.
definitions.example: the heat of summer

definitions.definition: Year; used to give the age of a person, usually a young one.
definitions.example: He was barely eighteen summers old.

definitions.definition: Someone with light, pinkish skin that has a blue undertone, light hair and eyes, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
A pack-horse.
A horizontal beam supporting a building.
A person who sums.
A machine or algorithm that sums.
In the summer.
A long vowel.
A long syllable.
A note formerly used in music, one half the length of a large, twice that of a breve.
A long integer variable, twice the size of an int, two or four times the size of a short, and half of a long long.
An entity with a long position in an asset.
definitions.example: Every uptick made the longs cheer.

definitions.definition: A long-term investment.
The long summer vacation at the English universities.
To take a long position in.
Having much distance from one terminating point on an object or an area to another terminating point (usually applies to horizontal dimensions; see Usage Notes below).
definitions.example: It's a long way from the Earth to the Moon.

definitions.definition: Having great duration.
definitions.example: The pyramids of Egypt have been around for a long time.

definitions.definition: Seemingly lasting a lot of time, because it is boring or tedious or tiring.
Not short; tall.
Possessing or owning stocks, bonds, commodities or other financial instruments with the aim of benefiting of the expected rise in their value.
definitions.example: I'm long in DuPont;  I have a long position in DuPont.

definitions.definition: Of a fielding position, close to the boundary (or closer to the boundary than the equivalent short position).
(of a ball or a shot) Landing beyond the baseline, and therefore deemed to be out.
definitions.example: That forehand is long.

definitions.definition: Occurring or coming after an extended interval; distant in time; far away.
A hard black enamel varnish containing asphalt.
Lacquerware.
To varnish with japan.
To hit with a whip.
definitions.example: The rider whipped the horse.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To hit with any flexible object.
definitions.example: I whipped her with a newspaper.

definitions.definition: To defeat, as in a contest or game.
To mix in a rapid aerating fashion, especially food.
definitions.example: to whip eggs or cream

definitions.definition: To urge into action or obedience.
definitions.example: He whipped the department into shape.

definitions.definition: To enforce a member voting in accordance with party policy.
To bind the end of a rope with twine or other small stuff to prevent its unlaying: fraying or unravelling.
To hoist or purchase by means of a whip.
To sew lightly; specifically, to form (a fabric) into gathers by loosely overcasting the rolled edge and drawing up the thread.
definitions.example: to whip a ruffle

definitions.definition: To throw or kick an object at a high velocity.
definitions.example: He whipped the ball at me.

definitions.definition: To fish a body of water especially by making repeated casts.
To snap back and forth like a whip.
definitions.example: The pennants whipped in the wind.

definitions.definition: To move very fast.
definitions.example: The wind whipped through the valley.

definitions.definition: To move (something) very fast; often with up, out, etc.
To transfer momentum from one skater to another.
To lash with sarcasm, abuse, etc.
To thrash; to beat out, as grain, by striking.
definitions.example: to whip wheat

definitions.definition: Controlled by a spouse or significant other to an unreasonable degree.
definitions.example: He knew he was whipped when he turned down a football game so they could shop for linens.

definitions.definition: A sensory perception of something that does not exist, often arising from disorder of the nervous system, as in delirium tremens; a delusion.
The act of hallucinating; a wandering of the mind; an error, mistake or blunder.
Ultraviolet colour.
Of electromagnetic radiation beyond (higher in frequency than) light visible to the human eye; radiation with wavelengths from 380 nanometre - 10 nanometre
To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.
definitions.example: Calculate the square root of 3 to 10 decimal places.

definitions.definition: To determine values or solutions by a mathematical process; reckon.
To plan; to expect; to think.
To ascertain or predict by mathematical or astrological computations the time, circumstances, or other conditions of; to forecast or compute the character or consequences of.
definitions.example: to calculate or cast one's nativity

definitions.definition: To adjust for purpose; to adapt by forethought or calculation; to fit or prepare by the adaptation of means to an end.
definitions.example: to calculate a system of laws for the government and protection of a free people

definitions.definition: Arrived at or determined by mathematical calculation; ascertained mathematically.
definitions.example: The calculated total appeared to be wrong.

definitions.definition: Carefully thought out or planned.
definitions.example: We made a calculated decision not to visit them on the first day, in case we seemed too eager.

definitions.definition: A person who is very old.
A person who lived in ancient times.
One of the senior members of the Inns of Court or of Chancery.
A senior; an elder; a predecessor.
A flag, banner, standard or ensign.
The bearer of a flag; ensign
The people of classical antiquity, especially such writers and philosophers.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
Having a tendency to seek revenge when wronged, vengeful.
Punitive
An alcoholic liquor distilled from fermented grain and usually aged in oak barrels.
A drink of this liquor.
A light gig or carriage.

Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.
definitions.example: The only reason why I haven't gone to university is because I can't afford it.

definitions.definition: Time spent in visual communication with another party (as opposed to communication over the phone, via e-mail, instant messaging, etc.).
Time spent visibly at one's workplace to satisfy expectations about one's working hours, even if this is unneeded or unproductive.
To communicate with somebody using the FaceTime videotelephony software.
Consisting of a variety of ingredients or parts.
Having diverse characteristics, abilities or appearances.
Any spherical (or nearly spherical) object.
definitions.example: the globe of the eye; the globe of a lamp

definitions.definition: The planet Earth.
A spherical model of Earth or any planet.
A light bulb.
A circular military formation used in Ancient Rome, corresponding to the modern infantry square.
(chiefly in the plural) A woman's breast.
A group.
To become spherical.
To make spherical.
One who plays at a game of chance, who gambles.
One who takes significant risks.
A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.
A medium for expression of talent or views.
A liquid content (e.g. oil) which acts as a binding and drying agent in paint. (FM 55-501).
(pharmaceuticals) The main excipient (such as an oil or gel) that conveys the active ingredient of a drug.
An entity to achieve an end.
A mode or method of spiritual practice; a yana.
An animal or (rarely) a plant on which a Hindu deity rides or sits
The note on which a musical key is based; the tonic.
The main theme of a speech, a written work, or a conference.
A speech that sets the main theme of a conference or other gathering; a keynote speech or keynote address.
To distort or collapse under physical pressure; especially, of a slender structure in compression.
To make bend; to cause to become distorted.
To give in; to react suddenly or adversely to stress or pressure (of a person).
definitions.example: It is amazing that he has never buckled after so many years of doing such urgent work.

definitions.definition: To yield; to give way; to cease opposing.
To enter upon some labour or contest; to join in close fight; to contend.
To buckle down; to apply oneself.
A clasp used for fastening two things together, such as the ends of a belt, or for retaining the end of a strap.
The brisure of an eighth daughter.
(roofing) An upward, elongated displacement of a roof membrane frequently occurring over insulation or deck joints. A buckle may be an indication of movement with the roof assembly.
A distortion, bulge, bend, or kink, as in a saw blade or a plate of sheet metal.
A curl of hair, especially a kind of crisp curl formerly worn; also, the state of being curled.
A contorted expression, as of the face.
To fasten using a buckle.
To unite in marriage.
(physical) An area; somewhere within an area.
A location or position in space.
A particular location in a book or document, particularly the current location of a reader.
A passage or extract from a book or document.
A topic.
A frame of mind.
definitions.example: I'm in a strange place at the moment.

definitions.definition: A chess position; a square of the chessboard.
(social) A responsibility or position in an organization.
A fortified position: a fortress, citadel, or walled town.
Numerically, the column counting a certain quantity.
definitions.example: three decimal places;  the hundreds place

definitions.definition: Ordinal relation; position in the order of proceeding.
definitions.example: That's what I said in the first place!

definitions.definition: Reception; effect; implying the making room for.
An emission of digestive gases from the anus; a flatus.
(impolite) An irritating person; a fool.
(impolite, potentially offensive) (usually as "old fart") An elderly person; especially one perceived to hold old-fashioned views.
(impolite) To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.
(usually as "fart around") To waste time with idle and inconsequential tasks; to go about one's activities in a lackadaisical manner; to be lazy or over-relaxed in one's manner or bearing.
To emit (fumes, gases, etc.).
A simple-minded person lacking common sense.
To stare or gape stupidly.
To stare conspicuously.
Of poor quality.
Strange, odd.
Unresponsive (of a software application’s user interface), sluggish.
A small, quick bite taken with the front teeth.
(in the plural, nibbles) Small snacks such as crisps/potato chips or nuts, often eaten to accompany drinks.
To eat with small, quick bites.
definitions.example: The rabbit nibbled at the lettuce.

definitions.definition: To bite lightly.
definitions.example: He nibbled at my neck and made me shiver.

definitions.definition: To consume gradually.
To find fault; to cavil.
To harden organic matter by permeating with water and depositing dissolved minerals.
To produce rigidity akin to stone.
To immobilize with fright.
To become stone, or of a stony hardness, as organic matter by calcareous deposits.
To become stony, callous, or obdurate.
To make callous or obdurate; to stupefy; to paralyze; to transform; as by petrification.
To state (something) with less completeness than needed; to minimise or downplay.
To state (something) with a lack of emphasis, in order to express irony.
To state a quantity that is too low.
Restrained and unpretentious.
A unitary council.
A unitary matrix or operator.
Having the quality of oneness.
(of a system of government or administration) That concentrates power in a single body, rather than sharing it with more local bodies.

definitions.definition: (of an algebra) That contains an identity element.
(of a matrix or operator) Whose inverse is equal to its adjoint.
To get.
To gain, usually by one's own exertions; to get as one's own
definitions.example: He acquired a title.

definitions.definition: To contract.
To sample signals and convert them into digital values.
Developed after birth; not congenital.
A house, or similar structure within a tree, or several trees, built with light materials; wood, bamboo or aluminum are preferred construction materials.
A group of twelve items.
definitions.example: Fractions would be a little easier if we counted by twelves.

definitions.definition: A twelve-bore gun.
A jury (normally composed of twelve persons).
Police; law enforcement, especially a narcotics officer
The cardinal number occurring after eleven and before thirteen, represented in Arabic numerals as 12 and in Roman numerals as XII.
definitions.example: There are twelve months in a year.

definitions.definition: Law enforcement; the police
A measure of cold or heat, often measurable with a thermometer.
definitions.example: The temperature in the room dropped nearly 20 degrees; it went from hot to cold.

definitions.definition: An elevated body temperature, as present in fever and many illnesses.
definitions.example: You have a temperature. I think you should stay home today. You’re sick.

definitions.definition: A property of macroscopic amounts of matter that serves to gauge the average intensity of the random actual motions of the individually mobile particulate constituents. http//arxiv.org/pdf/physics/0004055
The state or condition of being tempered or moderated.
The balance of humours in the body, or one's character or outlook as considered determined from this; temperament.
To pack or fill very tightly.
Packed or filled tightly.
Not having a name; unnamed.
Whose name is unknown; unidentified or obscure.
Anonymous
Unable to be described or expressed.
Indefinitely large numerically, many.
definitions.example: There are numerous definitions of the word 'man'.

definitions.definition: Happening or appearing in the night; night-time; nocturnal.
definitions.example: A cobweb spread above a blossom Is sufficient to protect It from nightly chill. — Tyndall.

definitions.definition: Performing, occurring, or taking place every night.
definitions.example: The dog demanded to go out for his nightly walk.

definitions.definition: Used in the night.
A public or private establishment that is open late at night to provide entertainment, food, drink, music and/or dancing.
A strip club.
To visit a nightclub (or nightclubs) for entertainment.
definitions.example: Our first night in the big city we went out nightclubbing. The next morning we stayed in hung over.

definitions.definition: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.
definitions.example: I'm sorry. I don't understand.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.
definitions.example: I understand that you have a package for me?

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

synonyms: pick up what someone is putting down

definitions.definition: To move something heavy by force of men, without aid of levers, pulleys, machine, or tackles.
To assault or beat up a person.
To mishandle; to handle roughly; to mangle.
To control (a machine, vehicle, situation, etc.) by means of physical strength.
A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.
One of several mutually exclusive things which can be chosen.
The remaining option; something available after other possibilities have been exhausted.
Alternative rock
Relating to a choice between two or more possibilities.
definitions.example: an alternative proposition

definitions.definition: Other; different from something else.
Not traditional, outside the mainstream, underground.
Alternate, reciprocal.
The art of studying the different parts of any organized body, to discover their situation, structure, and economy.
The science that deals with the form and structure of organic bodies; anatomical structure or organization.
definitions.example: Animal anatomy is also called zootomy; vegetable anatomy, phytotomy; and human anatomy, anthropotomy.

definitions.definition: A treatise or book on anatomy.
(by extension) The act of dividing anything, corporeal or intellectual, for the purpose of examining its parts.
definitions.example: Burton's famous treatise, "The Anatomy of Melancholy"

definitions.definition: The form of an individual.
definitions.example: I went to the Venice beach body-building competition and noticed the competitor from Athens, and let me tell you, that's what I call classic Greek anatomy.

definitions.definition: The human body, especially in reference to the private parts.
A skeleton, or dead body.
The physical or functional organization of an organism, or part of it.
An attitude of consideration or high regard
definitions.example: He is an intellectual giant, and I have great respect for him.

definitions.definition: Good opinion, honor, or admiration
(always plural) Polite greetings, often offered as condolences after a death.
definitions.example: The mourners paid their last respects to the deceased poet.

definitions.definition: A particular aspect, feature or detail of something
definitions.example: This year's model is superior to last year's in several respects.

definitions.definition: Good will; favor
To have respect for.
definitions.example: She is an intellectual giant, and I respect her greatly.

definitions.definition: To have regard for something, to observe a custom, practice, rule or right.
definitions.example: I respect your right to hold that belief, although I think it is nonsense.

definitions.definition: To abide by an agreement.
definitions.example: They failed to respect the treaty they had signed, and invaded.

definitions.definition: To take notice of; to regard as worthy of special consideration; to heed.
(dated except in "respecting") To relate to; to be concerned with.
To regard; to consider; to deem.
To look toward; to face.
Hello, hi
A student; one who studies at school or college, typically having a scholarship.
A specialist in a particular branch of knowledge.
A learned person; a bookman.
An assailant.
To urge with violence.
Involving extreme force or motion.
definitions.example: A violent wind ripped the branch from the tree.

definitions.definition: Involving physical conflict.
definitions.example: We would rather negotiate, but we will use violent means if necessary.

definitions.definition: Likely to use physical force.
definitions.example: The escaped prisoners are considered extremely violent.

definitions.definition: Intensely vivid.
definitions.example: The artist expressed his emotional theme through violent colors.

definitions.definition: Produced or effected by force; not spontaneous; unnatural.
The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.
One whose occupation is journalism, originally only writing in the printed press.
A reporter, who professionally does living reporting on news and current events.
A machine which translates linear motion of wind to rotational motion by means of adjustable vanes called sails.
The structure containing such machinery.
A child's toy consisting of vanes mounted on a stick that rotate when blown by a person or by the wind.
A dunk where the dunker swings his arm in a circular motion before throwing the ball through the hoop.
A guitar move where the strumming hand mimics a turning windmill.
A breakdancing move in which the dancer rolls his/her torso continuously in a circular path on the floor, across the upper chest, shoulders and back, while twirling the legs in a V shape in the air.
Any of various large papilionid butterflies of the genus Byasa, the wings of which resemble the vanes of a windmill.
The false shower.
An imaginary enemy, but presented as real.
To rotate with a sweeping motion.
definitions.example: She ran down the hill, windmilling her arms with glee.

definitions.definition: Of a rotating part of a machine, to (become disengaged and) rotate freely.
definitions.example: The axle broke and the wheel windmilled in place briefly before careening through the wall.

definitions.definition: Not needed; unnecessary.
definitions.example: This new project of yours will only be a needless expense.

definitions.definition: Needlessly, without cause.
To make something flat or flatter.
definitions.example: As there was a lot of damage, we chose the heavy roller to flatten the pitch.

definitions.definition: To press one's body tightly against a surface, such as a wall or floor, especially in order to avoid being seen or harmed.
To knock down or lay low.
definitions.example: The prize fighter quickly flattened his challenger.

definitions.definition: To become flat or flatter; to plateau.
definitions.example: Prices have flattened out.

definitions.definition: To be knocked down or laid low.
To lower by a semitone.
To make vapid or insipid; to render stale.
To reduce (a data structure) to one that has fewer dimensions, e.g. a 2×2 array into a list of four elements.
To combine (separate layers) into a single image.
Made flat by something.
definitions.example: We removed the fallen tree from the flattened car.

definitions.definition: A valley, especially a long, narrow, steep valley, cut in rock by a river.
The state of having complete legal control of something; possession; proprietorship.
Responsibility for something.
definitions.example: The successful candidate will take ownership of all internal design projects.

definitions.definition: An interval of twelve semitones spanning eight degrees of the diatonic scale, representing a doubling or halving in pitch frequency.
definitions.example: The melody jumps up an octave at the beginning, then later drops back down an octave.

definitions.definition: The pitch an octave higher than a given pitch.
definitions.example: The bass starts on a low E, and the tenor comes in on the octave.

definitions.definition: A coupler on an organ which allows the organist to sound the note an octave above the note of the key pressed (cf sub-octave)
A poetic stanza consisting of eight lines; usually used as one part of a sonnet.
The eighth defensive position, with the sword hand held at waist height, and the tip of the sword out straight at knee level.
The day that is one week after a feast day in the Latin rite of the Catholic Church.
An eight-day period beginning on a feast day in the Latin rite of the Catholic Church.
An octonion.
Any of a number of coherent-noise functions of differing frequency that are added together to form Perlin noise.
The subjective vibration of a planet.
Consisting of eight; eight in number.
An element of decoration; that which embellishes or adorns.
A Christmas tree decoration.
A musical flourish that is unnecessary to the overall melodic or harmonic line, but serves to decorate or "ornament" that line.
(in the plural) The articles used in church services.
A characteristic that has a decorative function (typically in order to attract a mate)
To decorate.
definitions.example: We will ornament the windows with trim to make the room seem brighter.

definitions.definition: To add to.
definitions.example: The editor ornamented his plain writing, making it fancier but less clear.

definitions.definition: A fruit or vegetable which has ripened.
To ripen or mature
(of fruits, vegetables, seeds etc.) Ready for reaping or gathering; having attained perfection; mature
definitions.example: ripe apples

definitions.definition: (of foods) Advanced to the state of fitness for use; mellow
definitions.example: ripe cheese

definitions.definition: Having attained its full development; mature; perfected
Maturated or suppurated; ready to discharge; -- said of sores, tumors, etc.
Ready for action or effect; prepared.
Like ripened fruit in ruddiness and plumpness.
Intoxicated.
Of a conflict between parties, having developed to a stage where the conflict may be reviewed by a court of law.
Smelly: having a disagreeable odor.
To define again or differently.
(archaic outside of idioms) To count, reckon, or enumerate.
definitions.example: All told, there were over a dozen.  Can you tell time on a clock?  He had untold wealth.

definitions.definition: To narrate.
definitions.example: I want to tell a story;  I want to tell you a story.

definitions.definition: To convey by speech; to say.
definitions.example: Finally, someone told him the truth.  He seems to like to tell lies.

definitions.definition: To instruct or inform.
definitions.example: Please tell me how to do it.

definitions.definition: To order; to direct, to say to someone.
definitions.example: Tell him to go away.

definitions.definition: To discern, notice, identify or distinguish.
definitions.example: Can you tell whether those flowers are real or silk, from this distance?  No, there's no way to tell.

definitions.definition: To reveal.
definitions.example: Time will tell what became of him.

definitions.definition: To be revealed.
To have an effect, especially a noticeable one; to be apparent, to be demonstrated.
definitions.example: Sir Gerald was moving slower; his wounds were beginning to tell.

definitions.definition: To use (beads or similar objects) as an aid to prayer.
To inform someone in authority about a wrongdoing.
definitions.example: I saw you steal those sweets! I'm going to tell!

definitions.definition: (authorship) To reveal information in prose through outright expository statement -- contrasted with show
definitions.example: Maria rewrote the section of her novel that talked about Meg and Sage's friendship to have less telling and more showing.

definitions.definition: Having force, or having a marked effect; weighty, effective.
definitions.example: a telling blow

definitions.definition: Revealing information; bearing significance.
definitions.example: a telling smile

definitions.definition: Serving to convince.
definitions.example: telling evidence

definitions.definition: A rebate.
A reduction in pay or conditions as a result of unfavourable economic conditions.
Any of several plants of the genus Lens, especially Lens culinaris, from southwest Asia, that have edible, lens-shaped seeds within flattened pods.
The seed of these plants, used as food.
To restrict; not to allow to go beyond a certain bound, to set boundaries.
definitions.example: I'm limiting myself to two drinks tonight.

definitions.definition: To have a limit in a particular set.
definitions.example: The sequence limits on the point a.

definitions.definition: To beg, or to exercise functions, within a certain limited region.
definitions.example: a limiting friar

definitions.definition: An express train that only halts at a limited number of stops.
With certain (often specified) limits placed upon it.
Of numbers, amounts, data: poor, small, felt to be insufficient.
definitions.example: I have a limited understanding of quantum physics.

definitions.definition: The style in which someone's hair has been cut and arranged.
To expose to the weather, or show the effects of such exposure, or to withstand such effects.
(by extension) To sustain the trying effect of; to bear up against and overcome; to endure; to resist.
To break down, of rocks and other materials, under the effects of exposure to rain, sunlight, temperature, and air.
To pass to windward in a vessel, especially to beat 'round.
definitions.example: to weather a cape; to weather another ship

definitions.definition: To endure or survive an event or action without undue damage.
definitions.example: Joshua weathered a collision with a freighter near South Africa.

definitions.definition: To place (a hawk) unhooded in the open air.
Weather, especially favourable or fair weather.
Mechanical or chemical breaking down of rocks in situ by weather or other causes.
A slight inclination given to an approximately horizontal surface to enable it to throw off water.

A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.
A weapon designed with the aforementioned specifications intended for slashing and/or stabbing and too short to be called a sword. A dagger.
Any blade-like part in a tool or a machine designed for cutting, such as that of a chipper.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
One who works in or with lead.
One who furnishes, fits, and repairs pipes and other apparatus for the conveyance of water, gas, or drainage.
A person who investigates or prevents leaks of information
In the Royal Navy, an apprentice, a boy aged 16 to 18, who is trained in technical skills at the Dockyard Schools to become an artificer.
A urologist.
A prosecuting attorney.
definitions.example: Annie Jay was the Wisconsin government prosecutor in the trial of a man for forging his client's signature.

definitions.definition: A person, as a complainant, victim, or chief witness, who institutes prosecution in a criminal proceeding.
definitions.example: The prosecutor got the witness to admit he was lying.

definitions.definition: Limitless or without bounds; unrestricted
A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Consistent; conforming to one standard.
With speed of convergence not depending on choice of function argument; as in uniform continuity, uniform convergence
(of a polymer) Composed of a single macromolecular species.
(of a polyhedron) That is isogonal and whose faces are regular polygons; (of an n-dimensional (n>3) polytope) that is isogonal and whose bounding (n-1)-dimensional facets are uniform polytopes.
Having meaning, significant.
definitions.example: I think we made a meaningful contribution to this project today.

definitions.definition: To join or unite (e.g. one thing to another, or as several particulars) so as to increase the number, augment the quantity or enlarge the magnitude, or so as to form into one aggregate.
To sum up; to put together mentally.
definitions.example: to add numbers

definitions.definition: To combine elements of (something) into one quantity.
definitions.example: to add a column of numbers

definitions.definition: To give by way of increased possession (to someone); to bestow (on).
To append (e,g, a statement); to say further information.
To make an addition; to augment; to increase.
definitions.example: It adds to our anxiety.

definitions.definition: To perform the arithmetical operation of addition.
definitions.example: He adds rapidly.

definitions.definition: To summon minions or reinforcements.
definitions.example: Typically, a hostile mob will add whenever it's within the aggro radius of a player.

definitions.definition: To cause to grow old; to impart the characteristics of age to.
definitions.example: Grief ages us.

definitions.definition: To postpone an action that would extinguish something, as a debt.
definitions.example: Money's a little tight right now, let's age our bills for a week or so.

definitions.definition: To categorize by age.
definitions.example: One his first assignments was to age the accounts receivable.

definitions.definition: To grow aged; to become old; to show marks of age.
definitions.example: He grew fat as he aged.

definitions.definition: The process of becoming older or more mature.
The deliberate process of making something (such as an antique) appear (or, in science fiction, become) older than it is.

Becoming senescent; accumulating damage to macromolecules, cells, tissues and organs with the passage of time; progressing loss of health, mobility, vibrancy and body functionality, resulting in biological death.
Wear, wear and tear.
Becoming elderly.
definitions.example: The aging artist could no longer steadily hold the brush.

definitions.definition: Excessive or superior power.
A sovereign state with dominant status on the globe and a very advanced military, especially the Soviet Union or United States.
A fictional extraordinary physical or mental ability, especially possessed by a superhero or supervillain.
Electricity generated in a large plant that is tied into a regional network, on a larger scale than was common in the early years of commercial electricity production.
(mathematics) A tetration (also known as a power tower and the inverse of a superlogarithm).
The property of a person of having high ideals that are usually unrealizable or at odds with practical life.
The practice or habit of giving or attributing ideal form or character to things; treatment of things in art or literature according to ideal standards or patterns;—opposed to realism.
An approach to philosophical enquiry, which asserts that direct and immediate knowledge can only be had of ideas or mental pictures.
Grandmother
A farmer; one who grows things.
definitions.example: He was an orange grower from Florida.

definitions.definition: Something that grows.
definitions.example: These flowers are fast growers

definitions.definition: Someone or something who becomes more likeable over time
definitions.example: I didn't like the song at first, but it is a real grower.

definitions.definition: A man whose penis does not show its full size until it is erect.
A large wasp, of the genus Vespa, having a brown-and-yellow-striped body and the ability to inflict a serious sting.
A person who pesters with petty but ceaseless attacks.
Any word used as the key to a code.
Any word used in a reference work to link to other words or other information.
A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.
Any word that occurs in a text more often than normal.
To tag with keywords, as for example to facilitate searching.
The state of being kind.
An instance of kind or charitable behaviour.
definitions.example: How can I thank you for your many kindnesses?

definitions.definition: A foolish person; an idiot.
definitions.example: Don't stick your fingers in the fan, you nimrod!

definitions.definition: The flow of blood through the circulatory system of an animal
A person who uses a puppet.
Someone who is manipulative and thus able to get people to do what they want or events to develop in the way they want, respectively, in a puppet-like manner.
To control a person, event, or organisation.
(with the verb "to be") Present in the vicinity.
definitions.example: Is Clare around today?

definitions.definition: (with the verb "to be") Alive; existing.
definitions.example: "How is old Bob? I heard that his health is failing."  "Oh, he's still around. He's feeling better now."

definitions.definition: So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.
definitions.example: High above, vultures circled around.

definitions.definition: So as to surround or be near.
definitions.example: Everybody please gather around.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; about.
definitions.example: An adult elephant weighs around five tons.

definitions.definition: From place to place.
definitions.example: Look around and see what you find.

definitions.definition: From one state or condition to an opposite or very different one; with a metaphorical change in direction; bringing about awareness or agreement.
definitions.example: He used to stay up late but his new girlfriend changed that around.

definitions.definition: (with turn, spin etc.) So as to partially or completely rotate; so as to face in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: She spun around a few times.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate repeated or continuous action, or in numerous locations or with numerous people.
definitions.example: I asked around, and no-one really liked it.

definitions.definition: Used with certain verbs to suggest unproductive activity.
Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.
definitions.example: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.

definitions.definition: (of abstract things) Centred upon; surrounding.
definitions.example: There has been a lot of controversy around the handling of personal information.

definitions.definition: Following the perimeter of a specified area and returning to the starting point.
definitions.example: She went around the track fifty times.

definitions.definition: Following a path which curves near an object, with the object on the inside of the curve.
definitions.example: The road took a brief detour around the large rock formation, then went straight on.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity of.
definitions.example: I don't want you around me.

definitions.definition: At or to various places within.
definitions.example: She went around the office and got everyone to sign the card.

definitions.definition: Decomposition into components in order to study (a complex thing, concept, theory etc.).
The result of such a process.
The mathematical study of functions, sequences, series, limits, derivatives and integrals.
Proof by deduction from known truths.
The process of breaking down a substance into its constituent parts, or the result of this process.
The analytical study of melodies, harmonies, sequences, repetitions, variations, quotations, juxtapositions, and surprises.
Psychoanalysis.
A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.
definitions.example: a campaign rally

definitions.definition: A protest or demonstration for or against something, but often with speeches and often without marching, especially in North America.
definitions.example: a political rally

definitions.definition: A sequence of strokes between serving and scoring a point.
An event in which competitors drive through a series of timed special stages at intervals. The winner is the driver who completes all stages with the shortest cumulative time.
A recovery after a decline in prices (said of the market, stocks, etc.)
Good-humoured raillery.
To fit or clasp together securely.
To interlace.
In a thorough or complete manner.
definitions.example: He went out in the rain and came back thoroughly drenched.

definitions.definition: An aircraft that is borne along by one or more sets of long rotating blades which allow it to hover, move in any direction including reverse, or land; and typically having a smaller set of blades on its tail that stabilize the aircraft.
definitions.example: We flew over the city in a helicopter.

definitions.definition: A powered troweling machine with spinning blades used to spread concrete.
The winged fruit of certain trees, such as ash, elm, and maple.
A dragonfly.
A whirling trick performed with devil sticks.
To transport by helicopter.
To travel by helicopter.
To rotate like a helicopter blade.
definitions.example: helicoptering his jacket, helicoptering his arms

definitions.definition: To overprotect one's children.
An especially large or powerful person, animal, or thing.
A platform-mounted machine for drilling rock.
Especially large or powerful.
A set amount of travelling, seen as a single unit; a discrete trip, a voyage.
Any process or progression likened to a journey, especially one that involves difficulties or personal development.
definitions.example: my journey of dealing with grief

definitions.definition: A day.
A day's travelling; the distance travelled in a day.
A day's work.
The weight of finished coins delivered at one time to the Master of the Mint.
(collective) A group of giraffes.
To travel, to make a trip or voyage.
Casually calm and relaxed.
definitions.example: We handled the whole frenetic situation with a nonchalant attitude.

definitions.definition: Indifferent; unconcerned; behaving as if detached.
definitions.example: He is far too nonchalant about such a serious matter.

definitions.definition: One whose actions make the headlines of news reports; one who affects the course of public discourse.
Something used or included temporarily or as a substitute for something that is not known or must remain generic; that which holds, denotes or reserves a place for something to come later.
definitions.example: This data is a placeholder, so you'll want to include the real numbers as soon as you have them.
